frameworks.name: trial-to-trial variability quenching
frameworks.one_line: stimulus onset reduces spiking variability across identical trials

frameworks.name: thalamocortical comparative electrophysiology
frameworks.one_line: contrasts variability dynamics between thalamic nuclei and cortex

core_question: Does stimulus-evoked variability quenching occur in thalamic nuclei as it does in cortex?
key_assumptions: trial-to-trial spike-count variability is a meaningful neural state index
fixation periods provide comparable pre-stimulus baselines across regions
manipulations: recording site: ventral pulvinar, dorsal pulvinar, LGN, area V4
epoch: pre-stimulus fixation vs post-stimulus presentation
key_findings: pre-stimulus variability lower in pulvinar and LGN than in V4
V4 shows prominent variability reduction at stimulus onset
thalamic variability largely unaffected by visual stimulation
small but significant variability decrease in dorsal pulvinar only
interpretations: variability quenching is a predominantly cortical phenomenon
thalamic nuclei operate in a distinct, less stimulus-modulated variability regime
